title: Minuartia cataractarum
taxonomy: Minuartia cataractarum is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is a type of dwarf cinquefoil, native to North America. This species is classified as a perennial herb, with small white flowers and narrow leaves. The classification of Minuartia cataractarum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the class Magnoliopsida. It is a member of the genus Minuartia, which is a group of small, low-growing plants.

intro: Minuartia cataractarum, also known as Alpine Pearlwort, is a perennial plant species in the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia cataractarum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues, and to promote wound healing.
